_comment: Committed load baseline (testing-plan Part 8 / Goal T10). Ceilings/floors come from the measured local run recorded in _measured below, plus margin; check-baseline.py fails a run that breaches them. Regenerate with `make load` (writes scripts/load/report/summary.json) and only widen a bound with a documented reason. Absolute latency numbers are host-relative -- the point is regression detection against THIS baseline, not a universal SLA.

_measured._comment: Actual numbers from the calibration run on the WSL2 dev host (2026-07-22, 150 subscribers), recorded so future runs compare against reality and not just against the ceilings.

sse_fanout._comment: growth_per_sub ~4 is BY DESIGN, not a leak: StreamJob opens one Redis subscription per connection, so each held stream costs roughly the handler goroutine + the go-redis connection read-loop + the sub.Channel() pump. Measured 3.97; the ceiling of 5 leaves margin while still catching a real per-connection regression (one extra goroutine per subscriber would breach it). The LEAK detector is goroutine_residual_max -- linear growth under load is expected, failing to return to baseline after release is not.

dispatch._comment: Backlog drain (Phase C): jobs submitted while the printer is offline queue onto the jobs:pending Redis Stream; when the printer returns, the consumer group must drain them. The ceiling is time-to-zero-backlog, so a dispatcher that stops keeping up -- or stalls entirely -- fails the run.
